Ports


Functions

CSynErr CSyn_SetPortSignalType (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName, uint32 partNum, CSyn_SignalType signalType)
 Set the signal type for a port.

int32 CSyn_GetPortSignalType (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName, uint32 partNum)
 Return value set by CSyn_SetPortSignalType().

CSynErr CSyn_SetPortAt (CSynContext context, int32 tick, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, double value)
 Set the value of a port at the specified time.

CSynErr CSyn_SetPort (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, double value)
 Set the value of a port.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadPort (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, double *valuePtr)
 Write the current value of a port to the address specified by valuePtr.

double CSyn_GetPort (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum)
 CSyn_GetPort() differs from CSyn_ReadPort() in that it does not use a pointer to return the value.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadPortFrames (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, int *valuePtr)
 Returns the total number of frames processed on this data queue.

int CSyn_GetPortFrames (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum)
 CSyn_GetPortFrames() differs from CSyn_ReadPortFrames() in that it does not use a pointer to return the value.

CSynErr CSyn_GetNumParts (CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName)
 Return number of parts that a Port has, or a negative error.

Function Documentation

CSynErr CSyn_GetNumParts (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  portName
) 
 

Return number of parts that a Port has, or a negative error.

For example, CSYN_UNIT_Line_Out's input port has two parts numbered 0 and 1.

double CSyn_GetPort (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  port,
uint32  partNum
) 
 

CSyn_GetPort() differs from CSyn_ReadPort() in that it does not use a pointer to return the value.

This is useful for calling from Java which does not like passing pointers.

int CSyn_GetPortFrames (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  port,
uint32  partNum
) 
 

CSyn_GetPortFrames() differs from CSyn_ReadPortFrames() in that it does not use a pointer to return the value.

This is useful for calling from Java which does not like passing pointers. Returns negative one on error which is ambiguous.

int32 CSyn_GetPortSignalType (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  portName,
uint32  partNum
) 
 

Return value set by CSyn_SetPortSignalType().

CSynErr CSyn_ReadPort (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  port,
uint32  partNum,
double *  valuePtr
) 
 

Write the current value of a port to the address specified by valuePtr.

The value will be converted to human range based on the port's signalType.

CSynErr CSyn_ReadPortFrames (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  port,
uint32  partNum,
int *  valuePtr
) 
 

Returns the total number of frames processed on this data queue.

Port must be either an envelope or a sample data port.

CSynErr CSyn_SetPort (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  port,
uint32  partNum,
double  value
) 
 

Set the value of a port.

The value will be converted to internal values based on the port's signalType. Only ports of type CSYN_PORT_TYPE_INPUT and CSYN_PORT_TYPE_VARIABLE can be set using this function.

CSynErr CSyn_SetPortAt (  CSynContext  context,
int32  tick,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  port,
uint32  partNum,
double  value
) 
 

Set the value of a port at the specified time.

CSynErr CSyn_SetPortSignalType (  CSynContext  context,
CSynToken  unit,
CSynName  portName,
uint32  partNum,
CSyn_SignalType  signalType
) 
 

Set the signal type for a port.

The signal type determines how the value specified using CSyn_SetPort() is converted to an internal numeric value. Setting the signal type allows one to specify a port value in more human values such as Hertz. This function is generally used if you are using an arithmetic unit to control something like the frequency of a state variable filter. You can set the port on the arihtmetic unit to CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SVF_FREQ and then pass Hz values. Acceptable values for signalType are:
CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_RAW_SIGNED,
CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_RAW_UNSIGNED,
CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_OSC_FREQ,
CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SAMPLE_RATE,
CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_HALF_LIFE,
CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_TIME,
and CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SVF_FREQ.

Generated on Tue Oct 5 17:58:09 2004 for CSYN by 1.3
